bioprojects.title: Genome sequence resource of Magnaporthe oryzae laboratory strain 2539

description.comment: Magnaporthe species cause blast disease on more than 50 kinds of monocot plants, including important crops such as rice, barley, millet and most recently wheat. Additionally, it is also an important model system for studying host-pathogen interaction. Here, we report a highly complete genome assembly and annotation of a laboratory strain 2539, which is a widely used progeny of rice-infecting isolate and grass-infecting isolate. The genome sequence resource of strain 2539 will be useful for studying the evolution, host adaption and pathogenicity of Magnaporthe species, which will be benefit for better understanding the mechanisms of host-pathogen interaction.

bioprojects.title: Pyricularia oryzae Genome sequencing and assembly

attributes.name: note
attributes.value: Takan et al., 2012 (Magnaporthe oryzae populations adapted to finger millet and rice exhibit distinctive patterns of genetic diversity, sexuality and host interaction. Mol. Biotechnol. 50 (2), 145-158)

description.comment: The fungus Magnaporthe oryzae causes blast disease of finger millet wherever the crop is mainly grown. In the east African region, finger millet serves as an important staple for millions of people. The aim of this research is to develop a reference genome of the finger millet blast fungus to investigate the adaptive evolutionary patterns associated with the biogeographic diversity of the pathogen populations in east Africa. For this purpose, we have selected M. oryzae isolate K23/123 for which multiple characteristics such as the AFLP-haplotype, mating type, sexual reproductive behaviour and pathogenicity have been established. The overall goal of the research is to develop new knowledge and resources that will contribute to the identification, development, and deployment of finger millet varieties with improved resistance to the blast disease.
description.title: Magnaporthe oryzae (Finger millet blast) -K23/123

bioprojects.title: Genome sequencing of Pyricularia oryzae (syn. Magnaporthe oryzae) Br62 blast fungus isolate

description.comment: Eleusine infecting isolate of P. oryzae
description.title: Br62_WGS

assembly_info.comments:
For whole-genome sequencing and assembly generation of the Br62 isolate, we extracted high molecular weight DNA following (A. Jones et al. 2021). Sequencing runs were then performed by Future Genomics Technologies (Leiden, The Netherlands) using the PromethION sequencing platform (Oxford Nanopore Technologies, Oxford, UK). Long reads were assembled into contigs and corrected using Flye (v2.9-b17680) (Kolmogorov et al. 2019) and polished with long reads using Medaka (v1.7.2)
(https://github.com/nanoporetech/medaka), and using short Illumina reads (San Diego, USA) through two consecutive iterations of Pilon (v1.23) (Walker et al. 2014).

bioprojects.title: Genome sequence analysis of highly avirulent isolate of Magnaporthe oryzae for comparative studies.

description.comment: Rice blast caused by Magnaporthe oryzae is the major rice yield threatening disease in India. North-Western Himalayan region is one of the main rice blast hotspots of India. The genome sequencing of avirulent isolate of M. oryzae Mo-nwi-55 from this region will help us to find the reasons associated with frequent loss of resistance in released rice varieties over few years and to also study the evolutionary forces acting at the genome level.
description.title: Magnaporthe oryzae strain Mo-nwi-55

assembly_info.submitter: National Research Centre on Plant Biotechnology
